mnemonics: NCLIVD - Numeric, Character, Logical, Integer, Vector, Data.Frame (A mnemonic for the first basic types you'll encounter, like a Roman numeral list)
keyPoints: Vectors are the fundamental data structure and must contain elements of the same type.
A data frame is a list of vectors of equal length, making it the primary structure for storing datasets.
Use the class() function to check the data type of an object.
commonMistakes: Using = instead of <- for assignment, which can cause issues in function calls.
Forgetting that factors are stored as integers, which can lead to confusion when performing numeric operations on them.
Assuming a numeric value is an integer; by default, numbers are stored as doubles (numeric).
